headline: The S&P 500’s 1.5% Selloff Is Driven By A Few Key Factors
summary: The S&P 500 closed at 666.06 on Thursday, falling 1.52% as Iranian strikes on two oil tankers sent crude prices surging toward $100 a barrel, reigniting inflation fears and triggering a broad selloff that spared almost no sector except energy. Reuters described it as the S&P 500’s biggest three-day percentage drop in a month, with ... The S&P 500’s 1.5% Selloff Is Driven By A Few Key Factors

headline: Trump tariffs: Martin Heinrich bill would give families tax rebate for higher import costs
summary: The Supreme Court in a 6-3 ruling Feb. 20 ruled that President Donald Trumps IEEPA tariffs were illegal.
